message:
Use artist sloppiness as default for arrows and polygons

Arrow and polygon examples now default to roughness: 1 (Artist style)
instead of roughness: 0 (Architect style), matching Excalidraw's native
hand-drawn appearance. This ensures generated diagrams have the
characteristic sketchy look rather than perfectly precise lines.

🤖 Generated with Claude Code

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,10 @@
+---
+title: Artist sloppiness as default for arrows and polygons
+type: bugfix
+authors:
+  - mavam
+  - claude
+created: 2026-01-06T06:28:02.947284Z
+---
+
+Arrow and polygon examples now use `roughness: 1` (Artist) instead of `roughness: 0` (Architect), matching Excalidraw's default hand-drawn style. This ensures generated diagrams have the characteristic sketchy appearance rather than clean, precise lines.

@@ -20,7 +20,7 @@ Lines and arrows with bindings and routing.
   "startArrowhead": null,
   "endArrowhead": "triangle",
   "elbowed": false,
-  "roughness": 0,
+  "roughness": 1,
   "roundness": null
 }
 ```

@@ -158,7 +158,7 @@ All fill and stroke styles work with polygons:
   "strokeColor": "#1971c2",
   "strokeWidth": 2,
   "strokeStyle": "solid",
-  "roughness": 0
+  "roughness": 1
 }
 ```
